abstract: Imbalanced learning represents one of the most persistent and formidable challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite decades of continuous advancement, the difficulty of learning from skewed class distributions remains a critical bottleneck, often limiting the practical utility of both traditional machine learning and modern deep learning models in real-world scenarios. Recent scholarship has made significant strides in this field, deepening our understanding of its fundamental nature while simultaneously uncovering new complexities. Given the rapid evolution of this landscape, this paper provides a comprehensive synthesis of recent breakthroughs and an in-depth review of existing strategies to mitigate class imbalance. Moving beyond traditional surveys that focus almost exclusively on classification, we expand our scope to include emerging techniques for regression tasks and the nuances of deep long-tail learning. Furthermore, we map the diverse practical impact of these methods across applications ranging from management science to engineering. Finally, we identify and discuss nascent challenges that define the next frontier of research in imbalanced learning.
